sanskrit:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम्।
पितृणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम्॥
transliteration:
anantaś cāsmi nāgānāṃ varuṇo yādasām aham |
pitṝṇām aryamā cāsmi yamaḥ saṃyamatām aham ||
literal_meaning: Among the Nāgas I am Ananta; among water-beings I am Varuṇa; among the ancestors I am Aryamā; among regulators I am Yama.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the supreme instances within four domains—serpents, aquatics, ancestors, and enforcers of cosmic law—showing that every layer of existence, even its guardians and punishers, is an expression of the same Self.
psychological_state: A moment of ego-dissolution in which the personal ‘I’ recognizes that even death, judgment, and ancestral debt are not external punishers but facets of one’s own wholeness.
trigger_scenario: Standing before a courtroom, a hospital bed, or a family graveyard and suddenly realizing that the authority you dread is an archetype within your own psyche.
anchor_text: See the One Self shining through every rank of beings.
